cipher.n_token: AES-256-GCM (96-bit IV). Keystream = AES-CTR with counter block iv(12)||be32(i); GCM J0 = iv||be32(1) (call0 input 0^128 -> output = GHASH subkey H), encryption counters start at i=2. DISTINCT third key, built by WASM export `rc` (window.hsw(jwt)); SOLVED — see docs/19-ntoken-cipher-solved.md and `n_token_master` below.

n_token_master.recovery: hcaptcha.hsw_n_token_decrypt.recover_ntoken_master_live() — deobf-capture the n-token AES fn's bitsliced round-key array (arg0, 120 words) on its first call, invert the fixslice key schedule (M[:16]=inv_bitslice(rk0); M[16:]=inv_bitslice(shift_rows_1(sub_bytes_nots(rk1)))).
n_token_master.verified: TWO independent checks: (1) full AES-256 key schedule of the recovered master reproduces all 120/120 captured round-key words; (2) AES-256(M, iv||be32(counter)) == the live cipher's actual keystream output for 198/198 blocks (token1) and 118/118 blocks of an INDEPENDENT second token (different iv) -> proves third-party decryption.
n_token_master.note: The master is a BUILD CONSTANT (same for every n-token of a given asset build) and rotates when the asset build rotates, like the vc encrypt/decrypt keys. The VALUE above is for build b6f4201d; re-run recover_ntoken_master_live() per build. The recovery is self-verifying (returns None unless 120/120 schedule match), so it never emits a fabricated key.

verified.hsw_n_key: SOLVED — see n_token_master. Recovered + verified against the live keystream (120/120 schedule + 198/198 + independent-token 118/118 blocks). The OLD `hsw.n_key` value below is a different (unrelated) capture and is NOT the n-token key.
verified.hsw_n_key_note: RESOLVED. The n-token AES-256 master is recovered by inverting the fixslice round-key array the cipher reads (recover_ntoken_master_live). The `rc`-path key is expanded into deobf-obfuscated bitsliced round keys; capturing arg0 (the rk array) on the n-token AES fn and inverting the schedule yields the master, self-verified by a 120/120 schedule match. See docs/19 Update 7.

hsw_n_key_meta.note: Captured at the AES key-schedule input on the path reachable from pc (the Promise executor export) but NOT from vc (the encrypt_req_data/decrypt_resp_data dispatcher). The static-across-calls property (same bytes captured every time the helper fires) structurally identifies this as the n-token's AES master key. Decrypt verification of the live n-token with this key fails under standard AES-GCM (iv||ct||tag and ct||tag||iv) and AES-CTR — the n-token uses a non-standard outer envelope or post-encrypt transform that's still being investigated. The KEY ITSELF is correct (it's the input to the actual AES.encrypt invoked by the bundle for n-token production). See docs/12-hsw-complete-summary.md.
